{"step_by_step_thinking": "T-lymphocytes, also known as T cells, are a type of white blood cell that plays a crucial role in the immune system. They are primarily involved in cell-mediated immunity and are responsible for recognizing and responding to foreign antigens. While T-lymphocytes can indirectly influence the production of antibodies by activating B cells, their primary functions include producing lymphokines, participating in delayed hypersensitivity reactions, and activating the complement system. However, the question specifically asks about the primary role of T-lymphocytes, and among the given options, the production of lymphokines and delayed hypersensitivity is more directly associated with T-lymphocytes.", "answer_choice": "B"}
